response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers. However, that systems work was in DLang, not the required modern C++ or Rust — his C++ exposure is a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis — and his recent years are dominated by TypeScript/Node.js full-stack product work. He has no routing, tiling, or navigation guidance experience, which is a core requirement, so he would be a stretch hire betting on transferable systems and geospatial skills.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of GISCollective and Green Map GIS work
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, caching, fibers)
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and demonstrated willingness to learn across the stack
concerns: Minimal modern C++ (short 2014-2015 role) and no Rust — the primary required skill is weak and dated
No routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance system experience
Recent 4+ years are TypeScript/Node.js web product work, far from C++/Linux data pipeline engineering
